description: Representing one's true nature or beliefs; not false or copied. 'Noemi' names the quality of self-authenticity—being genuinely what one is, without pretense or performance.
sound_symbolism: The word rings genuine. 'No' begins with grounded presence, self established. The vowel 'e' opens to expression, truth emerging. Then 'mi' completes with intimate certainty, authenticity confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'noemi kau' (authentic voice). Related to 'shewo' (true) but emphasizes faithfulness to inner nature rather than correspondence with external reality.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Authentic living aligns outer action with inner truth—the practice of integrity between being and doing.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Authentic communication expresses genuine feeling and need—pretense blocks true connection.
